title: Viva Mars Vegas

jokes.text: That's a lot of deniros. Oh, this 'a Berry Berry bad!
jokes.setup: Character sees eight million dollars and tries to use slang
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'De Niros' (slang for money) with Robert De Niro and then says 'Berry Berry' instead of 'very very'
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg malapropism - perfectly fits his character's tendency to mangle language and references

jokes.text: Look out, penny slots. I've got a system. It's to put all my money in you.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claims to have a gambling system
jokes.punchline: His 'system' is just losing all his money
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with Zoidberg's characteristic lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: What? It's full of fat guys in sandals.
jokes.setup: Professor warns Vegas isn't good for people like Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg misunderstands, thinking it's about physical appearance rather than gambling addiction
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's obliviousness to the real concern creates good misdirection

jokes.text: Oh, yes, better. A lonely weekend in my dumpster with a jar of pennies and tears.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests Zoidberg might be better off staying behind
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's pathetically detailed description of his sad alternative plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg pathos - the specific detail of 'pennies and tears' elevates it

jokes.text: You mean the one in that alley off 72nd street? Yeah. What do you think I'm pointing at?
jokes.setup: Robot mafia needs to ditch loot in 'that dumpster down there'
jokes.punchline: Overly specific address for something they're just pointing at randomly
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity creates a nice contradiction between vague pointing and precise address

jokes.text: God, it's Zoidberg. I hate to bother you, but... Okay, okay, I'll shut up!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg addresses God while finding the money
jokes.punchline: He immediately backs down as if God told him to shut up
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character work - Zoidberg's instant submission to an imagined divine rebuke

jokes.text: This very lucky casino own. Someone wins one million dollar every hour! Usually it us.
jokes.setup: Casino owner brags about how lucky his casino is
jokes.punchline: Reveals that they're the ones who usually win
jokes.explanation: Standard casino joke but well-delivered with Wong's broken English adding character

jokes.text: Well, they do work for minimum wage in a land that was once theirs. Yeah, but they never made wise use of the land.
jokes.setup: Amy points out the injustice of native Martians working for minimum wage on their own land
jokes.punchline: Wong dismisses this with typical colonizer logic
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of colonialism that perfectly fits Wong's character as an oblivious capitalist

jokes.text: When my ancestor Reginald wong found them here, they had no bingo parlors and only one prostitute.
jokes.setup: Wong explains why the Martians didn't make good use of the land
jokes.punchline: His criteria for 'good use' are bingo parlors and prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of colonizer narratives - absurd values presented as civilization

jokes.text: Pfft. Pathetic.
jokes.setup: Wong's ridiculous justification for colonization
jokes.punchline: Amy sarcastically agrees, playing along with her father's twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction that shows Amy playing along with her father's absurdity

jokes.text: Ooh, perfect timing! It's the weekly changing of the shrimp!
jokes.setup: Wong shows off the buffet
jokes.punchline: He's excited about replacing week-old shrimp as if it's a grand ceremony
jokes.explanation: Parodies the 'changing of the guard' ceremony with disgusting buffet maintenance

jokes.text: It's Mars' happiest place on earth!
jokes.setup: Wong describes his casino
jokes.punchline: Says it's on Earth when they're clearly on Mars
jokes.explanation: Simple geographical confusion joke, fairly predictable

jokes.text: This much money could feed a small, starving planet. But it doesn't!
jokes.setup: Bender observes the massive amount of money could help people
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully notes that it doesn't, with obvious satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - acknowledges suffering then celebrates not helping, excellent economy

jokes.text: Don't mess with blind Joe. Nothing get past his sense of smell. Yeah, that's why he in here. Guarding galaxy's most secure safe.
jokes.setup: Wong brags about their security guard's amazing sense of smell
jokes.punchline: The implication that a blind guard who relies on smell is guarding money (which doesn't smell)
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about using the wrong sense for the job

jokes.text: Category-five sucker now arriving.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg arrives at the casino with money
jokes.punchline: Announcement treats gamblers like weather disasters with a classification system
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor connecting hurricane categories to gambling marks, very economical

jokes.text: ♪ the minute you walked in the joint, I could smell you were a man who was stinkin' ♪
jokes.setup: Parody of a seductive casino lounge song
jokes.punchline: Instead of romantic attraction, it's about literal bad smell
jokes.explanation: Good parody structure that maintains the rhythm while subverting the content

jokes.text: It's my eight million dollars. God gave it to me.
jokes.setup: Friends question where Zoidberg got the money
jokes.punchline: His completely literal interpretation that finding money means God gave it to him
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's naive religious interpretation fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, come now! God didn't get to be God by giving away money.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claims God gave him money
jokes.punchline: Professor applies business logic to divinity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor line - treats God like a CEO, perfect economy and character voice

jokes.text: This is why you never see a poor person with millions of dollars
jokes.setup: Discussion about taking money with you when you die
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's completely backwards logic about poverty and wealth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that's completely wrong but sounds profound - peak Zoidberg wisdom

jokes.text: I think that's about eight.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg requests eight one-million-dollar chips
jokes.punchline: Worker's casual uncertainty about counting to eight when dealing with millions
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan delivery about basic counting with huge stakes

jokes.text: Zoidberg, what are you doing?! I don't know. I think it's called roulette.
jokes.setup: Amy is shocked at Zoidberg's reckless gambling
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg doesn't even know what game he's playing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's complete gambling ignorance

jokes.text: Let it ride!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg just won $288 million
jokes.punchline: Instead of taking the money, he immediately bets it all again
jokes.explanation: Classic gambling escalation but fits Zoidberg's character perfectly, great economy

jokes.text: Vodka with a sardine in it, please. In fact, vodka with a sardine all around!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is celebrating his big win
jokes.punchline: His idea of a fancy celebration drink is vodka with sardines for everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - his wealth doesn't change his gross tastes, 'sardini' is a nice touch

jokes.text: No! I'm having fun and a sardini!
jokes.setup: Amy begs him to set aside money for an apartment
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg refuses and coins the term 'sardini' for his signature drink
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that creates a signature Zoidberg cocktail name, good wordplay

jokes.text: Now that's the God I know.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg loses everything on a bad roll
jokes.punchline: Professor confirms his earlier theory about God not giving away money
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier God joke, perfectly timed for maximum impact

jokes.text: But for one brief moment, I made everybody on Mars happy. And if you ask me, that makes me a winner.
jokes.setup: Amy points out he threw away a fortune
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg finds a philosophical silver lining to his massive loss
jokes.explanation: Sweet Zoidberg moment that's both touching and pathetic, good character development

jokes.text: Category-five loser now departing.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg leaves the casino broke
jokes.punchline: Reverses the earlier 'category-five sucker' announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend to the earlier announcement, shows his complete reversal of fortune

jokes.text: This Judith Krantz novel's only missing its frontispiece.
jokes.setup: Clamps needs to find something to break over Zoidberg's head
jokes.punchline: He evaluates a romance novel's completeness before using it as a weapon
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail - a violent robot being precious about book condition

jokes.text: He can't be lying', boss, not after a krantzing like that.
jokes.setup: Clamps hit Zoidberg with a Judith Krantz novel
jokes.punchline: They turn the author's name into a verb for their torture method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that creates a new verb from the author's name - very creative wordplay

jokes.text: We should have brought a gun.
jokes.setup: Robot mafia's elaborate murder plan is thwarted by ink
jokes.punchline: Simple realization that basic weapons would have worked better
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation about overcomplicating simple tasks

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Amy's mirror got a tattoo!
jokes.setup: Amy looks in a mirror after a wild weekend
jokes.punchline: She thinks the mirror itself got tattooed, not her
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about who has the tattoo, shows Amy's hangover confusion

jokes.text: Oh, Hermes, you and your preposterous Sci-Fi nonsense. No, this is simply a translucidating ink polarizer.
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses Hermes' sci-fi explanation
jokes.punchline: Then gives an even more ridiculous sci-fi explanation himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor hypocrisy - mocks sci-fi while spouting even more technical nonsense

jokes.text: Nobody said that.
jokes.setup: Assumption that his tattoo removal would be less painful than regular methods
jokes.punchline: Professor matter-of-factly clarifies it will be just as painful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor deadpan - maximum comedy with minimal words, great timing

jokes.text: Hide a giant lobster covered in ink? And how do you propose we do that?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks them to hide him from the robot mafia
jokes.punchline: Hermes points out the obvious impossibility of hiding something so conspicuous
jokes.explanation: Sets up the invisibility revelation, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You mean I'm invisible? No, no, no. Not in any sense of the word. But essentially, yes, entirely.
jokes.setup: Professor explains how the ink polarizer made Zoidberg invisible
jokes.punchline: Professor's contradictory explanation that he's not invisible but completely invisible
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor double-talk - contradicts himself while being technically correct

jokes.text: It's no use, Zoidberg. We're, like, 85% sure you're in there!
jokes.setup: Robot mafia can't find the invisible Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: They give up but with oddly specific confidence about his presence
jokes.explanation: The specific percentage makes their failure funnier, good absurd precision

jokes.text: Come on, clamps, let's go, and watch out... there's some sort of smelly air pocket over here.
jokes.setup: Robot mafia is leaving after failing to find Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: They notice Zoidberg's smell but don't connect it to his presence
jokes.explanation: Good irony that they detect him but miss the obvious conclusion

jokes.text: What are you, an idiot? Pretty much, yeah.
jokes.setup: Donbot insults Clamps' intelligence
jokes.punchline: Clamps cheerfully agrees with the insult
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment, Clamps' honesty about his stupidity

jokes.text: Your casino... give it to us. Okay, fine, I just retire to my fabulous mansion.
jokes.setup: Mafia makes threatening takeover demand
jokes.punchline: Wong immediately caves and acts like it's a pleasant retirement plan
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Wong's pragmatic cowardice disguised as optimism

jokes.text: What's rent?
jokes.setup: Parents reveal they've been paying Amy's rent
jokes.punchline: Amy doesn't even know what rent is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment showing her extreme privilege and sheltered life

jokes.text: It's not a dump. It's the company housing we built for our employees.
jokes.setup: Someone calls their new living situation a dump
jokes.punchline: Wong reveals this was actually employee housing they provided
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on corporate treatment of workers, shows Wong's oblivious callousness

jokes.text: You can't afford tissue any more. This is Walgreens brand nose paper.
jokes.setup: Amy thanks 'tissue ghost' (invisible Zoidberg)
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg corrects her about the quality of tissue they can afford now
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing how far they've fallen, Zoidberg being precise about cheap brands

jokes.text: No, you snuck in here while I was grabbing a bubble cake.
jokes.setup: Amy accuses Zoidberg of sneaking in while she bathed
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's ridiculous counter-claim about 'bubble cake'
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's weird food reference adds to the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: Of course, because no one can possibly know what I'm up to.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests using his invisibility for a heist
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg sarcastically agrees while presumably doing something obvious
jokes.explanation: Standard invisible person irony, sets up visual gag we can't see

jokes.text: Um, how come we've never been in this room before? I think this chart will answer that.
jokes.setup: Amy questions the sudden appearance of a chart room
jokes.punchline: Professor deflects with a chart that presumably explains plot convenience
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about TV show convenience and the need for exposition scenes

jokes.text: Not if Zoidberg eats the money. Everything he swallows turns invisible.
jokes.setup: Problem of how to steal visible money while invisible
jokes.punchline: Professor's solution involves Zoidberg eating hundreds of pounds of cash
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd solution that creates new problems, good escalation

jokes.text: He stinks like the inside of a tauntaun.
jokes.setup: Discussing how Zoidberg's smell might give him away
jokes.punchline: Specific Star Wars reference to describe his horrible odor
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference without much twist, but vivid and appropriate

jokes.text: Whoa-ho! Don't shut that door, partner. I'm a cr-razy Texas robot millionaire, and I plan on losing billions in your 'casiny.'
jokes.setup: Bender needs to distract the Donbot from closing the safe
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top Texas accent and mispronounces 'casino' as 'casiny'
jokes.explanation: Good Bender disguise work, the accent and mispronunciation add character

jokes.text: Hello! I'm prime minister Nelson Okeke of Nigeria. I trust you received my spam e-mail?
jokes.setup: Hermes arrives as another distraction
jokes.punchline: He's pretending to be a Nigerian email scammer in person
jokes.explanation: Good internet culture reference with Hermes doing the role perfectly

jokes.text: Uh, I said 'aseeka kanamoo atall.' It means, uh, 'we must leave now in an unusual manner.'
jokes.setup: Professor needs to cover for Zoidberg's distressed cry
jokes.punchline: Makes up fake foreign phrase with ridiculous translation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor improvisation, the fake translation is appropriately awkward

jokes.text: Look! The shrimp cart's paying out!
jokes.setup: Money falls from shrimp cart due to Zoidberg burping
jokes.punchline: Patron thinks the cart is a slot machine
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that fits the casino setting. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Smell with your eyes what I see with my nose.
jokes.setup: Blind Joe detects something wrong
jokes.punchline: He confuses his senses in this dramatic declaration
jokes.explanation: Excellent sensory confusion line that's both dramatic and nonsensical

jokes.text: And let this be a lesson to any other albino lobsters thinking of robbing our casino
jokes.setup: Donbot threatens to shoot the invisible Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: His warning is to a very specific and unlikely demographic
jokes.explanation: Great absurd specificity - treats Zoidberg as part of a larger demographic threat

jokes.text: This casino is wrongfully ours.
jokes.setup: Amy reveals casino belongs to Martians
jokes.punchline: Donbot's malapropism combining 'rightfully' and 'wrongfully'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism that captures the Donbot's confusion while being economical.

jokes.text: So, forever.
jokes.setup: Professor says invisibility lasts until Zoidberg takes a bath
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg realizes this means forever given his hygiene habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg self-awareness about his terrible hygiene, great economy

tmdb_overview: The crew stages a casino heist to recover stolen property from the Robot Mafia.
